ZEAL/BZea Stem-Pigment (StPi) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — FDR ≤ 0.05

Stem-anthocyanin analog of the DTA notebook: the same SNP50K ancestry pipeline, with the raw per-genotype stem-pigment score used directly (no spatial correction) as the phenotype and the maize anthocyanin-biosynthesis pathway as the candidate set. Significance is Benjamini–Hochberg FDR ≤ 0.05 (per-scan q-values): the Manhattan line is the −log₁₀P at which BH q = 0.05, and candidate/peak calls report q.

Author

Fausto Rodríguez-Zapata

Published

July 8, 2026

What this is. The stem-pigment (StPi) sibling of zeal-qtl-recovery-dta-mlm-snp50k-fdr, built from the same real BZea pipeline: field StPi → raw per-genotype mean (used directly) → SNP50K teosinte-ancestry dosage → RTIGER ancestry mosaic → K → JLM + MLM. The population is the same near-isogenic B73×teosinte BC2S3 lines from 5 teosinte taxa. StPi scores stem anthocyanin intensity (a 0–1 scale), i.e. how strongly the culm accumulates purple anthocyanin pigment; most plots score 0 with a pigmented minority, so the direct per-genotype mean is ≈ 0.05.

ImportantWhy the phenotype is used directly (no spatial correction)

StPi is a bounded 0–1 pigment score, not a continuous measurement. A SpATS genotype-fixed 2-D P-spline (used for the continuous DTA/DTS/PH traits) is the wrong model here — fitting it pushed BLUEs out of range (13 genotypes > 1, impossible for a 0–1 score). So StPi is analysed as the raw per-genotype mean of the plot scores (mean within each field, then across fields), with no spatial correction.

ImportantGenotype at 0.4× skim: why ancestry, not per-SNP

SNP50K is 51,991 teosinte-vs-B73 sites pileup-genotyped at ~0.4× — hard genotypes are unusable (70% missing, ~0% hom-ALT), so ancestry is inferred from the per-sample allele counts. The authentic per-SNP dosage (Panel B) is λ_GC-deflated (sparse mean-imputation); the RTIGER ancestry mosaic (Panel C) is the usable, block-smoothed genotype (teosinte presence 0.111, matching the established BzeaSeq ~0.101). Only the phenotype changes vs the DTA notebook.

Trait context. Stem pigmentation is an anthocyanin trait. In TeoNAM (Chen 2019) the pigment/color traits (e.g. glume color) had simple genetic architectures — a single large-effect QTL plus a few small-effect ones — unlike the polygenic flowering (DTA/DTS) or height (PH) traits. The candidates here are the classic maize anthocyanin-biosynthesis genes: the regulators r1/b1 (bHLH) and c1/pl1 (MYB) that switch the pathway on, and the structural enzymes c2 (CHS), chi1 (CHI), pr1 (F3’H), a1 (DFR), a2 (ANS), bz1 (UFGT) and bz2 (GST).

library(here)
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(fastman)
library(ggrepel)
library(ggtext)
library(scales)
library(cowplot)
source(here("scripts/teonam_notebook_plots.R")) # shared: plot_manhattan/lollipop, lambda_gc, LOD
mlm <- fread(here("data/zeal/stpi_gwas_mlm_mosaic_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel C
persnp <- fread(here("data/zeal/stpi_gwas_mlm_persnp_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel B
ols <- fread(here("data/zeal/stpi_gwas_ols_donor_accession_snp50k.csv"))[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
overlap <- here("results/sim/zeal/stpi_candidate_overlap.csv")
lam <- function(p) {
  p <- p[is.finite(p) & p > 0 & p <= 1]
  round(median(qchisq(p, 1, lower.tail = FALSE)) / qchisq(0.5, 1), 2)
}
ALPHA <- 0.05
fdr_lod <- function(p, alpha = ALPHA) {
  p <- p[is.finite(p) & p > 0]
  q <- p.adjust(p, "BH")
  if (!any(q <= alpha)) {
    return(NA_real_)
  }
  -log10(max(p[q <= alpha]))
}
qval <- function(p) p.adjust(p, "BH") # BH q-values aligned to p
NoteSignificance = Benjamini–Hochberg FDR ≤ 0.05

Every Manhattan’s dotted line is drawn at the −log₁₀P where BH q = 0.05 for that scan; a marker is significant iff its BH q ≤ 0.05. If no marker passes FDR, no line is drawn. Candidate/peak tables report q.

Candidate genes: closest marker vs window-max marker

For each maize anthocyanin candidate (B73 v5), the closest marker to the gene body vs the highest −log₁₀P marker within ±500 kb in the RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Taxon+K) scan (Panel C). pathway records the biosynthetic role (regulatory bHLH/MYB vs structural enzyme).

sc <- copy(mlm)[is.finite(P) & P > 0][, logP := -log10(P)][, q := qval(P)]
gg <- fread(here("data/teonam/stpi_candidate_genes.tsv"))
gg[, chr := as.integer(chr)]
WIN <- 5e5
ctab <- rbindlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(gg)), function(i) {
  ch <- gg$chr[i]
  s <- gg$start[i]
  e <- gg$end[i]
  m <- sc[CHR == ch]
  if (!nrow(m)) {
    return(NULL)
  }
  d <- pmax(0L, pmax(s - m$BP, m$BP - e))
  kc <- which.min(d)
  w <- m[BP >= s - WIN & BP <= e + WIN]
  kw <- if (nrow(w)) which.max(w$logP) else NA
  data.table(
    gene = gg$symbol[i], pathway = gg$pathway[i], chr = ch, `gene start (v5)` = s,
    `closest Δstart (bp)` = m$BP[kc] - s, `closest -log10P` = round(m$logP[kc], 2),
    `window-max -log10P` = if (nrow(w)) round(w$logP[kw], 2) else NA_real_,
    `window-max q (BH)` = if (nrow(w)) signif(w$q[kw], 2) else NA_real_,
    `FDR<=0.05` = if (nrow(w)) w$q[kw] <= ALPHA else NA
  )
}))
setorder(ctab, chr, `gene start (v5)`)
knitr::kable(ctab,
  format.args = list(big.mark = ","),
  caption = "StPi candidates (B73 v5, anthocyanin pathway): closest vs window-max (±500 kb) marker, RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Taxon+K) scan, with Benjamini–Hochberg q at the window-max marker."
)
StPi candidates (B73 v5, anthocyanin pathway): closest vs window-max (±500 kb) marker, RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Taxon+K) scan, with Benjamini–Hochberg q at the window-max marker.
gene pathway chr gene start (v5) closest Δstart (bp) closest -log10P window-max -log10P window-max q (BH) FDR<=0.05

chi1 structural (CHI) 1 300,070,598 893 0.15 0.26 0.950 FALSE
b1 regulatory (bHLH) 2 19,822,666 -31,978 0.96 1.25 0.530 FALSE
a1 structural (DFR) 3 221,779,777 -68,610 2.62 2.70 0.091 FALSE
c2 structural (CHS) 4 198,742,446 26,988 1.36 1.77 0.310 FALSE
a2 structural (ANS) 5 68,086,545 -34,467 0.35 1.07 0.630 FALSE
pr1 structural (F3’H) 5 183,698,956 26,113 1.04 1.30 0.510 FALSE
pl1 regulatory (MYB) 6 119,938,912 -3,155 0.04 0.08 0.990 FALSE
in1 regulatory (bHLH repressor) 7 19,934,845 3,972 0.00 0.16 0.970 FALSE
c1 regulatory (MYB) 9 11,117,066 28,501 1.49 1.76 0.310 FALSE
bz1 structural (UFGT) 9 13,120,306 431 0.97 1.01 0.670 FALSE
r1 regulatory (bHLH) 10 141,187,279 -8,000 0.11 0.37 0.920 FALSE

OLS → MLM: structure control on a pigment trait

The fixed-effect OLS (Taxon + marker, no K) vs the MLM (Taxon + K) on the RTIGER mosaic, same fixed part. OLS is intrinsically inflated (ancestry-block LD); K deflates λ toward 1.

plot_manhattan(ols,
  sprintf("ZEAL/BZea StPi — OLS (Taxon + marker, no K), RTIGER mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f; dotted = FDR 0.05)", lam(ols$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = fdr_lod(ols$P),
  out_png = here("results/sim/zeal/stpi_gwas_ols_manhattan_fdr_snp50k.png")
)
lam_tab <- data.table(
  model = c("OLS (Taxon + marker, no K)", "MLM (Taxon + K)"),
  markers = c(nrow(ols), nrow(mlm)),
  lambda_GC = c(lam(ols$P), lam(mlm$P)),
  `n FDR<=0.05` = c(sum(qval(ols$P) <= ALPHA), sum(qval(mlm$P) <= ALPHA)),
  max_logP = round(c(max(-log10(ols$P)), max(-log10(mlm$P))), 2)
)
knitr::kable(lam_tab, caption = "OLS vs MLM on the RTIGER mosaic (same Taxon fixed part): K deflates genomic inflation; n markers at BH FDR ≤ 0.05.")
OLS vs MLM on the RTIGER mosaic (same Taxon fixed part): K deflates genomic inflation; n markers at BH FDR ≤ 0.05.
model markers lambda_GC n FDR<=0.05 max_logP
OLS (Taxon + marker, no K) 48900 6.82 18078 8.74
MLM (Taxon + K) 48900 1.18 841 8.66 
ov <- fread(overlap)
peak_at <- function(scan, ch, s) {
  w <- scan[CHR == ch & abs(BP - s) <= 5e5 & is.finite(P) & P > 0]
  if (!nrow(w)) NA_real_ else round(max(-log10(w$P)), 2)
}
cmp <- ov[, .(
  gene = symbol, chr,
  `OLS peak` = mapply(peak_at, list(ols), chr, start),
  `MLM peak` = mapply(peak_at, list(mlm), chr, start)
)]
cmp[, `drop (OLS−MLM)` := round(`OLS peak` - `MLM peak`, 2)]
setorder(cmp, -`OLS peak`)
knitr::kable(cmp, caption = "Local peak -log10P (±500 kb) per candidate: OLS vs MLM.")
Local peak -log10P (±500 kb) per candidate: OLS vs MLM.
gene chr OLS peak MLM peak drop (OLS−MLM)
b1 2 7.02 1.25 5.77
c1 9 3.14 1.76 1.38
c2 4 2.89 1.77 1.12
bz1 9 2.39 1.01 1.38
r1 10 2.19 0.37 1.82
pr1 5 1.13 1.30 -0.17
a1 3 1.04 2.70 -1.66
bz2 1 0.98 0.34 0.64
chi1 1 0.60 0.26 0.34
a2 5 0.42 1.07 -0.65
in1 7 0.40 0.16 0.24
pl1 6 0.16 0.08 0.08

JLM QTL genomic distribution (Panel A)

StPi QTL from JLM (TASSEL StepwiseOLS, nested in taxon, enter/exit P=1e-4=LOD4). Model-independent — the primary analysis, as in Chen 2019.

p_lolli <- plot_lollipop(
  here("results/sim/zeal/tassel/stpi_jlm_native1.txt"), mlm,
  "ZEAL/BZea StPi — JLM QTL genomic distribution (nested in taxon)",
  overlap_csv = overlap, label_genes = c("r1", "b1", "c1", "pl1", "a1", "a2", "bz1", "bz2", "c2", "chi1", "pr1", "in1"),
  out_png = here("results/sim/zeal/stpi_jlm_lollipop_snp50k.png")
)
p_lolli

MLM (Taxon + K), RTIGER ancestry mosaic (Panel C)

p_mlm <- plot_manhattan(mlm,
  sprintf("ZEAL/BZea StPi — MLM (Taxon + K), RTIGER ancestry mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f; dotted = FDR 0.05)", lam(mlm$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = fdr_lod(mlm$P), out_png = here("results/sim/zeal/stpi_gwas_mlm_mosaic_manhattan_fdr_snp50k.png")
)
p_mlm
cat(sprintf("%d markers pass FDR <= 0.05 (BH); FDR line at -log10P = %s\n", sum(qval(mlm$P) <= ALPHA), ifelse(is.na(fdr_lod(mlm$P)), "NA (none)", sprintf("%.2f", fdr_lod(mlm$P)))))
841 markers pass FDR <= 0.05 (BH); FDR line at -log10P = 3.07

Composite (A: JLM · B: per-SNP MLM · C: RTIGER-mosaic MLM)

Panel B (authentic per-SNP dosage) is λ_GC-deflated but catches sharp SNP hits the mosaic block-terraces away. Panel C (the ancestry mosaic) is the usable, well-calibrated genotype.

p_B <- plot_manhattan(persnp,
  sprintf("StPi — MLM (Taxon+K), authentic per-SNP dosage (λ_GC = %.2f)", lam(persnp$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = fdr_lod(persnp$P)
)
p_C <- plot_manhattan(mlm,
  sprintf("StPi — MLM (Taxon+K), RTIGER ancestry mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f)", lam(mlm$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = fdr_lod(mlm$P)
)
composite <- cowplot::plot_grid(p_lolli, p_B, p_C, ncol = 1, labels = c("A", "B", "C"), label_size = 20, align = "v", axis = "lr")
ggsave(here("results/sim/zeal/stpi_composite_snp50k.png"), composite, width = 9, height = 13, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
composite

Multi-caller StPi scan (control · nNIL · RTIGER · LB-Impute · binhmm)

The same StPi MLM (Taxon+K) scan on each ancestry caller’s recovered genotypes, at the real ~0.4× depth. The robust, trait-independent finding is teosinte-presence recovery: the block/bin-aggregated callers (RTIGER 0.111; binhmm 0.101) recover the established BzeaSeq fraction (~0.10), while per-SNP calling (control) and the per-SNP HMM recalls (nNIL, LB-Impute) under-call. Genomic inflation is phenotype-dependent (reported per caller below). Caller genotypes are population-level and trait-independent; only the phenotype (StPi) changes.

callers <- c(control = "persnp", nNIL = "nnil", RTIGER = "mosaic", `LB-Impute` = "lbimpute", binhmm = "binhmm")
gg <- fread(here("data/teonam/stpi_candidate_genes.tsv"))[, chr := as.integer(chr)]
min_cand_q <- function(sc) {
  q <- qval(sc$P)
  vals <- unlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(gg)), function(i) {
    idx <- which(sc$CHR == gg$chr[i] & abs(sc$BP - gg$start[i]) <= 5e5)
    if (length(idx)) min(q[idx]) else NA_real_
  }))
  if (all(is.na(vals))) NA_real_ else signif(min(vals, na.rm = TRUE), 2)
}
ctab2 <- rbindlist(lapply(names(callers), function(nm) {
  g <- callers[[nm]]
  sc <- fread(here(sprintf("data/zeal/stpi_gwas_mlm_%s_donor_accession_snp50k.csv", g)))
  mf <- here(sprintf("data/zeal/zeal_%s_mosaic.rds", if (g == "mosaic") "rtiger" else g))
  pres <- if (file.exists(mf)) {
    st <- readRDS(mf)$state
    round(mean(st %in% c(1L, 2L), na.rm = TRUE), 3)
  } else {
    NA_real_
  }
  sc <- sc[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
  data.table(
    caller = nm, `teosinte presence` = pres, lambda_GC = lam(sc$P),
    `n FDR<=0.05` = sum(qval(sc$P) <= ALPHA),
    `best candidate q (BH)` = min_cand_q(sc)
  )
}))
knitr::kable(ctab2, caption = "StPi MLM (Taxon+K) by ancestry caller at 0.4×, with n markers at BH FDR ≤ 0.05 and the best (min) q across the anthocyanin candidate windows.")
StPi MLM (Taxon+K) by ancestry caller at 0.4×, with n markers at BH FDR ≤ 0.05 and the best (min) q across the anthocyanin candidate windows.
caller teosinte presence lambda_GC n FDR<=0.05 best candidate q (BH)
control NA 0.70 132 0.100
nNIL 0.018 0.90 10 0.570
RTIGER 0.111 1.18 841 0.091
LB-Impute 0.035 0.89 101 0.980
binhmm 0.101 0.79 22 0.300 
panels <- lapply(names(callers), function(nm) {
  sc <- fread(here(sprintf("data/zeal/stpi_gwas_mlm_%s_donor_accession_snp50k.csv", callers[[nm]])))
  plot_manhattan(sc, sprintf("StPi MLM (Taxon+K) — %s (λ_GC = %.2f; FDR 0.05)", nm, lam(sc$P)), overlap_csv = overlap, lod = fdr_lod(sc$P))
})
cc <- cowplot::plot_grid(plotlist = panels, ncol = 1, labels = LETTERS[1:5], label_size = 18, align = "v", axis = "lr")
ggsave(here("results/sim/zeal/stpi_caller_composite_fdr_snp50k.png"), cc, width = 9, height = 20, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
cc
